svg.text: SINHALA
Running Text — Four Passages + Bridge Phrase
— I. WACHANA — PROVERB —
ගෙදර ගස්සෙන් පෙනෙන්නට එය් යායි.
gedara gassenin penennaata ey yayi = home from-outside trying-to-see only go
"[Do not try to] understand your own home only from the outside." (Sinhala proverb on wisdom)
— II. SMARANA — ON MEMORY —
ඊතට ගිය ඉතිහාසය පිලිකේ කරන්නට ආකාරය්
iyata giya ithihasaya pililikee karannaata aakaara = those who-went history to-reflect way
"The way to illuminate history is to reflect on those who have gone before us."
— III. JNANA — ON KNOWLEDGE —
ගියාවීන් කගෙන් හැයියාම ල්ලාඨ්යෙන්
giyavin kagen haeyiyaa llaadyen = from those-who-went lighting burning = light taken from past
"The light is taken from those who have gone before." (Sinhala saying on inherited wisdom)
— IV. ANUWA — FORWARD —
ආපි ඉදිරියට යන්නට ඊතට ගියවුන් ස්මරන කරනා
aapit idiriyata yannata iitaata giyavun smarana karana
"While moving forward together, remembering those who went ahead." (guiding concept)
— V. BRIDGE PHRASE —
මම මීය ඉදිරියට කයා කරන්ලට ස්මරන කරන ග඼යන්ට
මීය ඒබට නිර෍මේළිය කරේළි.
mama mee idiiriyata kaya karanlaata smarana kara giyanta
mee oba ata nirmela kereeli = this for-you freely was-made
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered forward."
— BRIDGE PHRASE — NOTES —
Idiriya (ඉදිරිය) = forward/front/ahead. Nirmela = free of charge/pure/free.
Sinhala SOV structure places the main verb at the end: subject + object + verb.
The forward-memory concept in Sinhala connects to Buddhist concept of karmic continuity.
— LANGUAGE NOTES —
Sinhala: ~17 million L1 speakers; official language of Sri Lanka.
Language family: Indo-European, Indo-Aryan, insular branch (isolated from mainland).
Settled in Sri Lanka ~5th century BCE from northern India. Rich Pali Buddhist tradition.
Sinhala diverged significantly from other Indo-Aryan languages due to island isolation.
REMEMBER FORWARD — PLATE 44D — SINHALA: RUNNING TEXT — rememberforward.org — CC BY-SA 4.0
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
